title: Convert Claude Chat to PDF Online | Free & Private Tool
description: The safest way to convert Claude AI conversations to PDF online. Retain all text formatting, code blocks, and chat structure for professional archiving.
h1: Convert Claude Chat to PDF Online
intro: Native Claude exports often fail to preserve the readability of complex threads. Our online tool is engineered specifically to parse the Claude DOM and reconstruct it into a pristine, editorial-quality PDF document.
useCase: Best for researchers and developers who need to present AI reasoning steps in a static, professional document format without browser clutter.

sections.title: Convert Claude Share Link to PDF
sections.content: Have a Claude share link you want to keep forever? Simply paste your claude.ai/share/ URL into our converter and we'll extract the full conversation — including code blocks, formatting, and nested lists — and render it into a professionally styled PDF. No extensions, no sign-ups, and your data never leaves your browser when using Failsafe mode.

faqs.q: How does this differ from 'Print to PDF'?
faqs.a: Unlike standard browser printing, we use a custom CSS engine that prevents cut-offs in long code blocks and ensures headers and bullets remain perfectly aligned.

faqs.q: Can I convert Claude 3.5 Sonnet chats?
faqs.a: Yes, our engine is updated daily to support the latest HTML structure of all Anthropic Claude models.

faqs.q: Can I convert a Claude share link to PDF?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Paste any public claude.ai/share/ link into our Auto Extraction tab and we'll fetch, parse, and render the full conversation into a clean PDF — code blocks, lists, and all formatting preserved.

faqs.q: How do I export Claude chat to PDF?
faqs.a: Two ways: paste the public share link for automatic extraction, or use Failsafe Mode by viewing the page source (Ctrl+U), copying the HTML, and pasting it into our tool. Both methods produce identical high-fidelity PDFs.

faqs.q: Does Claude2PDF preserve formatting?
faqs.a: Yes. Our rendering engine preserves headings, bold/italic text, bullet and numbered lists, tables, inline code, and fenced code blocks exactly as they appear in the original Claude conversation.
